{{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者={{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4th year }} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kanei|ja|寛永}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

revision.text:
{{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者={{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=draft}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継|12=draft}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4th year|12=draft}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kanei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=draft}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>
== References == {{Reflist|group="脚注"|2}}
== Source Material == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yashima no Misasagi}} * [http://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/index.php?m=d&id=28 崇道天皇八島陵 - 観光スポット｜奈良市観光協会公式ホームページ]
<!--Yashima Mausoleum-->

== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継|12=draft}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4th year|12=draft}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kanei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=draft}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

{{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者={{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=draft}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継|12=draft}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4th year|12=draft}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kanei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=draft}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=draft}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継|12=draft}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4th year|12=draft}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kanei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=draft}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=draft}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>

He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国|12=draft}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=八島陵 |画像=[[ファイル:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[円墳]] |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[早良親王]] |出土品= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[画像:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|参道下より]] '''八島陵'''（やしまのみささぎ、やしまりょう）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]八島町にある[[早良親王]]の陵。'''八嶋陵'''とも記され、また親王が崇道天皇と追尊されたことより、'''崇道天皇陵'''とも呼ばれる。[[文久]]年間に治定された<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県、p.1113</ref>。
== 構造 == 陵域は[[延喜式]]の[[諸陵式]]によると「東西五町<ref group="脚注">約545m</ref>、南北四町<ref group="脚注">約436m</ref>、守戸二烟」、『奈良県報告』第8回御陵墓表によると「東西壱町壱分弐厘<ref group="脚注">約109m</ref>、南北参八間壱分七厘<ref group="脚注">約69m</ref>」と記されている<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編 p.193</ref>。形状はやや楕円形で、周辺には方形の土塀が廻り、松や広葉樹が繁茂するため遠目には方形墳のように見える<ref name="shishikoko193"/>。「[[陵墓要覧]]」では円墳と分類されている<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。
===八島陵前石室古墳=== [[画像:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|八つ石]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} 陵直前の道路内に、封土が失われ大型自然石の[[横穴式石室]]天井石が露出した古墳がある<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編 p.195</ref>。規模は南北長さ約7m、幅約3m<ref name="shishikoko195" />。近隣ではこの巨石群は'''八つ石'''と呼ばれており、言い伝えでは早良親王が淡路国での死に際し石を9つ投げ、落ちたところに葬って欲しいと告げて絶命した<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県、p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編 pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">大和国添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref>。それらのうち8つがこの地で見つかり、親王の陵が当地で造営されたと伝わる他、一説には八島の地名の由来ともなったとされる<ref name="kadokawa1112" /><ref name="fudoki" /><ref name="yatsuishi" /><ref group="脚注">八島町内に伝わる史料『大和国添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記』によると、早良親王薨去後、大伴朝臣友彦の夢に親王が現れたという。感激した友彦が淡路を訪ね墓所を三拝したところ、親王が現れ東方に9つの石を投げ、その留まるところに陵墓を遷せと告げられた。東国を尋ねたところ石の1つは大和国二見付近、残りは大和国古市付近に留まった。友彦がこのことを奏上したところ、勅使として参議[[春原五百枝|五百枝]]と友彦が淡路に遣わされ、御赦文が読み上げられた。友彦が親王の遺骨を負い大和に戻り、延暦17年（[[798年]]）戊寅3月、八ッ石の地に御陵を築いて納め奉ったという。</ref>。しかし実際には、古墳時代後期の造営と見られ<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編 p.196</ref>、早良親王とは直接の関係はないようである。石を取り除こうとすると工事関係者が死んだり病で倒れたりしたと言われ、祟りがあるとの伝承がある<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112" />。
== 歴史 == 早良親王は[[藤原種継]]暗殺に連座した疑いを受け、[[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]）に廃嫡、[[淡路国]]に配流の途上、35歳で[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]された。屍は淡路に送られ、最初の墓は淡路に造営されたが<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編 p.194</ref>、霊が祟りをなしたと恐れられたため、延暦19年（[[800年]]）に崇道天皇と追尊され、淡路の墓も山陵とし、淡路国[[津名郡]]の戸2烟に守らせたという<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。さらに延暦24年（[[805年]]）、改葬司を任じて八島陵への改葬が執り行われた<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju" /><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史、p.124</ref>。
[[寛永]]4年（[[1627年]]）の『[[宗国史]]』によると、八島の地には'''崇道天皇社'''と宮寺があったと記されている<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編 p.545</ref>。この宮寺は'''八島寺'''であったとされ、近世はこれらの社寺が八島陵の現在地及び周辺を占めていた<ref name="shishishaji545" />。『明治34年（[[1901年]]）[[添上郡]]郷村社神社祭神由緒調書<ref group="脚注">[[奈良県立図書情報館]]行政資料</ref>』に古図の写しが含まれており、そちらには八島陵の位置に拝殿・鳥居・社殿等、その西南に薬師堂・十羅刹跡・宮坊・土蔵などが描かれている<ref name="shishishaji546">奈良市史 社寺編 p.546</ref>。[[明治]]以降これらの寺社は移築あるいは廃され、現在の御陵が整備された<ref name="shishishaji545" />。
===崇道天皇社=== [[1886年]]（[[明治]]19年）の移築まで、現在の八島陵中心部に祀られていた<ref name="shishishaji532">奈良市史 社寺編 p.532</ref>。[[慶応]]元年（[[1865年]]）、この敷地が崇道天皇陵であると判明するまで、近傍12ヶ村の郷社として信仰を集めた<ref name="shishishaji532"/>。毎年8月には降雨を祈願しての一会が催され、法楽芸能として[[相撲]]や[[田楽]][[猿楽]]、[[念仏#称名念仏|踊り念仏]]などが郷民により奉納されたが、見物人の重みで仮屋の屋根が崩れた事もあるほど、群衆で賑わったという<ref name="kadokawa1112"/>。
本殿は式年造替に伴って[[春日大社]]の本殿を移築したものであったが<ref name="shishishaji533">奈良市史 社寺編 p.533</ref>、1886年、御陵の整備に伴い崇道天皇社は近傍の[[嶋田神社]]と合祀され、本殿も嶋田神社本殿として移築された<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。
===八島寺=== [[1875年]]（明治8年）に廃寺となるまで八島陵の西北にあり、八島陵前の八つ石にちなんで八石山と号し、東西およそ23.6m、南北およそ41.5mの寺地を有していた<ref name="shishishaji545546">奈良市史 社寺編 p.545-546</ref>。往古は八島の北、藤原村（現奈良市藤原町）の大門、堂ノ前等の字名<ref group="脚注">[http://koaza.nara-hgis.jp/ 奈良盆地歴史地理データベース]で確認する限り、近代までは伝わらなかった字名のようである。</ref>を持つ地域にあったと伝わる<ref>山陵廻之日記</ref><ref name="shishishaji545"/>。
早良親王の霊を鎮めるために建立された寺であり、延暦19年7月勅使を淡路国に派遣し、早良親王の遺骨を分けて添上郡今木庄（読み：いまきのしょう。現在の古市町）の南にあった大和国八島寺に奉納したとの記録が残る<ref name="shishishaji545"/><ref>帝王編年記</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>。別伝には延暦25年（[[806年]]）に、山階の地に八島寺を建立したとの記録もあり<ref>扶桑略記 延暦25年11月条</ref><ref>元亨釈書 延暦25年条</ref>、別称として山階寺を挙げる記録も残る<ref>大和志</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>。[[弘仁]]5年（[[814年]]）には、八島寺で1本の茎から18本の稲穂が出たという瑞祥が記録されている<ref>日本後紀 弘仁5年8月辛酉の条</ref><ref>元亨釈書</ref><ref name="shishishaji545"/><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>。本尊は薬師如来座像を安置していたと伝わる<ref name="shishishaji546"/>。また八島寺にあったとみられる古い[[涅槃仏|涅槃図]]が今日まで個人蔵として伝わるが、絹本著色で縦164cm、横118.5cmの大きなもので、箱に「八石山八嶌寺常什物」と記されており、裏書には[[享保]]7年（[[1732年]]）に改装されたと記されている<ref name="shishishaji546"/>。
明治になると[[廃仏毀釈]]の嵐の中八島寺も廃され、[[毘沙門天]]像が300円で売りに出された<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。この売上を元に[[昭和]]期に入って[[公民館]]と毘沙門堂が整備され、新しい毘沙門天像も買い入れられたと伝わる<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。
==周辺の崇道天皇神社== 近傍の町内には'''崇道天皇神社'''と名乗る小社がいくつか鎮座しており、八島陵内にかつて存在した崇道天皇社から勧請されて成立したものと見られる<ref name="chiisaki"/>。いずれも主祭神として早良親王を祀る。旧社のあった八島町及びこれらの小社のある町は全て中世の八島郷に含まれていたようである<ref name="fudoki"/><ref>大和志料</ref><ref group="脚注">八島郷の範囲は、興隆寺・鉢伏・鹿野園・古市・安場・虚空蔵・高樋・山村・窪ノ庄・池田・今市・南永井・北永井・出屋敷・神殿・八島・横井・藤原・田中の19村。</ref>。
===出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社=== {{神社 |名称 = 崇道天皇神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sudo Tenno Shrine Deyashiki Honden Far,jpg.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = 奈良県奈良市出屋敷町28 |祭神 = 崇道天皇（早良親王） |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[10月13日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |位置 = {{coord|34|39|47.2|N|135|49|44.4|E}} }} {{osm box|w|417323039|出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社}} 760平方メートルの境内東方に石鳥居があり、西に進むと南面する本殿が見える<ref name="shishishaji511">奈良市史 社寺編 p.511</ref>。本殿のみで境内社はない<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。周辺は境内にすぐ隣接して住宅地となるが、境内にはまだ多くの樹木が残り、遠方からでも森を望むことができる<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。玉垣外に一対の石灯籠があり、「[[天明]]元年（[[1781年]]） 崇道天王 清水永井」と刻まれている<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。玉垣内の石灯籠、狛犬は[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）製<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。
===神殿町 崇道天皇神社=== {{神社 |名称 = 崇道天皇神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sudo Tenno Shrine Kodono Haiden,jpg.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = 奈良県奈良市神殿町522 |祭神 = 崇道天皇（早良親王） |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = 銅板葺 |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |位置 = {{coord|34|39|31.8|N|135|49|24.0|E}} }} {{osm box|w|417320208|神殿町 崇道天皇神社}} 本殿は銅板葺で南面し、各部の金具はりっぱな造りになっている<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。古くから雨の神として信仰され、[[江戸時代|江戸期]]末の[[絵馬]]が残されている。境内社として[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]が祀られている他、本殿築地の外、拝殿の南の東側には稲荷神社もあり、[[保食神]]を祀っている<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。稲荷の北には[[地蔵菩薩|地蔵]]堂が安置され、小さな石仏が壇上に並ぶ。堂の傍らには五輪塔の火輪、蓮座等が置かれているが、隣接する神殿町公民館はかつて安養寺と呼ばれる寺院であったため、その遺物と思われる<ref name="shishishaji512">奈良市史 社寺編 p.512</ref>。
===北永井町 崇道天皇神社=== {{神社 |名称 = 崇道天皇神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sudo Tenno Shrine KitaNagai Haiden.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = 奈良県奈良市北永井町425 |祭神 = 崇道天皇（早良親王） |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |位置 = {{coord|34|39|21.6|N|135|49|30.0|E}} }} {{osm box|w|417317860|北永井町 崇道天皇神社}} 本殿は南面し、左右に市杵島神社（[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]）と八阪神社（[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]）が鎮座している<ref name="shishishaji512"/>。他に拝殿、土蔵等があり、境内はかなり広く、拝殿南の境内地にはすべり台など遊具が設けてある<ref name="shishishaji512"/>。[[宮座]]は元、東・中・西など五座があったが、のちに本[[頭屋|当屋]]・平当屋・新当屋などを中心に祭事が行われ、それも次第に簡略化されている<ref name="shishishaji512"/>。
==脚注== {{Reflist|group="脚注"|2}}
==出典== {{Reflist|2}}
==参考文献== * {{Cite book|和書|editor =「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会 竹内理三|year = 1990|title =[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 29 奈良県|page=|publisher = 角川書店|isbn = 4040012909}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=[[池田源太]]、[[宮坂敏和]]、奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=[[名著出版]]|year=1989|page=|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 考古編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1968|page=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1985|page=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=大和古代地名辞典|editor=[[池田末則]]、日本地名学研究所|publisher=[[五月書房]]|year=1998|page=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=〈小さき社〉の列島史|editor=[[牛山佳幸]]|publisher=平凡社|year=2000|page=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 続編|editor=[[山田熊夫]]|publisher=豊住書店|year=1979|page=68-69}}
==関連項目== * [[崇道天皇神社]] - 各地にある早良親王を祀る神社へのリンク。 * [[御霊神社]] * [[御霊信仰]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Yashima no Misasagi}} {{Commonscat|Emperor Sudo Shrines|崇道天皇神社}} * [http://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/index.php?m=d&id=28 崇道天皇八島陵 - 観光スポット｜奈良市観光協会公式ホームページ]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やしまのみささき}} [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[八島陵]] to [[Yashima Mausoleum]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=八島陵 |画像=[[ファイル:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[円墳]] |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[早良親王]] |出土品= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[画像:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|参道下より]] '''八島陵'''（やしまのみささぎ、やしまりょう）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]八島町にある[[早良親王]]の陵。'''八嶋陵'''とも記され、また親王が崇道天皇と追尊されたことより、'''崇道天皇陵'''とも呼ばれる。[[文久]]年間に治定された<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県、p.1113</ref>。
== 構造 == 陵域は[[延喜式]]の[[諸陵式]]によると「東西五町<ref group="脚注">約545m</ref>、南北四町<ref group="脚注">約436m</ref>、守戸二烟」、『奈良県報告』第8回御陵墓表によると「東西壱町壱分弐厘<ref group="脚注">約109m</ref>、南北参八間壱分七厘<ref group="脚注">約69m</ref>」と記されている<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編 p.193</ref>。形状はやや楕円形で、周辺には方形の土塀が廻り、松や広葉樹が繁茂するため遠目には方形墳のように見える<ref name="shishikoko193"/>。「[[陵墓要覧]]」では円墳と分類されている<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。
===八島陵前石室古墳=== [[画像:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|八つ石]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} 陵直前の道路内に、封土が失われ大型自然石の[[横穴式石室]]天井石が露出した古墳がある<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編 p.195</ref>。規模は南北長さ約7m、幅約3m<ref name="shishikoko195" />。近隣ではこの巨石群は'''八つ石'''と呼ばれており、言い伝えでは早良親王が淡路国での死に際し石を9つ投げ、落ちたところに葬って欲しいと告げて絶命した<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県、p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編 pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">大和国添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref>。それらのうち8つがこの地で見つかり、親王の陵が当地で造営されたと伝わる他、一説には八島の地名の由来ともなったとされる<ref name="kadokawa1112" /><ref name="fudoki" /><ref name="yatsuishi" /><ref group="脚注">八島町内に伝わる史料『大和国添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記』によると、早良親王薨去後、大伴朝臣友彦の夢に親王が現れたという。感激した友彦が淡路を訪ね墓所を三拝したところ、親王が現れ東方に9つの石を投げ、その留まるところに陵墓を遷せと告げられた。東国を尋ねたところ石の1つは大和国二見付近、残りは大和国古市付近に留まった。友彦がこのことを奏上したところ、勅使として参議[[春原五百枝|五百枝]]と友彦が淡路に遣わされ、御赦文が読み上げられた。友彦が親王の遺骨を負い大和に戻り、延暦17年（[[798年]]）戊寅3月、八ッ石の地に御陵を築いて納め奉ったという。</ref>。しかし実際には、古墳時代後期の造営と見られ<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編 p.196</ref>、早良親王とは直接の関係はないようである。石を取り除こうとすると工事関係者が死んだり病で倒れたりしたと言われ、祟りがあるとの伝承がある<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112" />。
== 歴史 == 早良親王は[[藤原種継]]暗殺に連座した疑いを受け、[[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]）に廃嫡、[[淡路国]]に配流の途上、35歳で[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]された。屍は淡路に送られ、最初の墓は淡路に造営されたが<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編 p.194</ref>、霊が祟りをなしたと恐れられたため、延暦19年（[[800年]]）に崇道天皇と追尊され、淡路の墓も山陵とし、淡路国[[津名郡]]の戸2烟に守らせたという<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。さらに延暦24年（[[805年]]）、改葬司を任じて八島陵への改葬が執り行われた<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju" /><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史、p.124</ref>。
[[寛永]]4年（[[1627年]]）の『[[宗国史]]』によると、八島の地には'''崇道天皇社'''と宮寺があったと記されている<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編 p.545</ref>。この宮寺は'''八島寺'''であったとされ、近世はこれらの社寺が八島陵の現在地及び周辺を占めていた<ref name="shishishaji545" />。『明治34年（[[1901年]]）[[添上郡]]郷村社神社祭神由緒調書<ref group="脚注">[[奈良県立図書情報館]]行政資料</ref>』に古図の写しが含まれており、そちらには八島陵の位置に拝殿・鳥居・社殿等、その西南に薬師堂・十羅刹跡・宮坊・土蔵などが描かれている<ref name="shishishaji546">奈良市史 社寺編 p.546</ref>。[[明治]]以降これらの寺社は移築あるいは廃され、現在の御陵が整備された<ref name="shishishaji545" />。
===崇道天皇社=== [[1886年]]（[[明治]]19年）の移築まで、現在の八島陵中心部に祀られていた<ref name="shishishaji532">奈良市史 社寺編 p.532</ref>。[[慶応]]元年（[[1865年]]）、この敷地が崇道天皇陵であると判明するまで、近傍12ヶ村の郷社として信仰を集めた<ref name="shishishaji532"/>。毎年8月には降雨を祈願しての一会が催され、法楽芸能として[[相撲]]や[[田楽]][[猿楽]]、[[念仏#称名念仏|踊り念仏]]などが郷民により奉納されたが、見物人の重みで仮屋の屋根が崩れた事もあるほど、群衆で賑わったという<ref name="kadokawa1112"/>。
本殿は式年造替に伴って[[春日大社]]の本殿を移築したものであったが<ref name="shishishaji533">奈良市史 社寺編 p.533</ref>、1886年、御陵の整備に伴い崇道天皇社は近傍の[[嶋田神社]]と合祀され、本殿も嶋田神社本殿として移築された<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。
===八島寺=== [[1875年]]（明治8年）に廃寺となるまで八島陵の西北にあり、八島陵前の八つ石にちなんで八石山と号し、東西およそ23.6m、南北およそ41.5mの寺地を有していた<ref name="shishishaji545546">奈良市史 社寺編 p.545-546</ref>。往古は八島の北、藤原村（現奈良市藤原町）の大門、堂ノ前等の字名<ref group="脚注">[http://koaza.nara-hgis.jp/ 奈良盆地歴史地理データベース]で確認する限り、近代までは伝わらなかった字名のようである。</ref>を持つ地域にあったと伝わる<ref>山陵廻之日記</ref><ref name="shishishaji545"/>。
早良親王の霊を鎮めるために建立された寺であり、延暦19年7月勅使を淡路国に派遣し、早良親王の遺骨を分けて添上郡今木庄（読み：いまきのしょう。現在の古市町）の南にあった大和国八島寺に奉納したとの記録が残る<ref name="shishishaji545"/><ref>帝王編年記</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>。別伝には延暦25年（[[806年]]）に、山階の地に八島寺を建立したとの記録もあり<ref>扶桑略記 延暦25年11月条</ref><ref>元亨釈書 延暦25年条</ref>、別称として山階寺を挙げる記録も残る<ref>大和志</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>。[[弘仁]]5年（[[814年]]）には、八島寺で1本の茎から18本の稲穂が出たという瑞祥が記録されている<ref>日本後紀 弘仁5年8月辛酉の条</ref><ref>元亨釈書</ref><ref name="shishishaji545"/><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>。本尊は薬師如来座像を安置していたと伝わる<ref name="shishishaji546"/>。また八島寺にあったとみられる古い[[涅槃仏|涅槃図]]が今日まで個人蔵として伝わるが、絹本著色で縦164cm、横118.5cmの大きなもので、箱に「八石山八嶌寺常什物」と記されており、裏書には[[享保]]7年（[[1732年]]）に改装されたと記されている<ref name="shishishaji546"/>。
明治になると[[廃仏毀釈]]の嵐の中八島寺も廃され、[[毘沙門天]]像が300円で売りに出された<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。この売上を元に[[昭和]]期に入って[[公民館]]と毘沙門堂が整備され、新しい毘沙門天像も買い入れられたと伝わる<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>。
==周辺の崇道天皇神社== 近傍の町内には'''崇道天皇神社'''と名乗る小社がいくつか鎮座しており、八島陵内にかつて存在した崇道天皇社から勧請されて成立したものと見られる<ref name="chiisaki"/>。いずれも主祭神として早良親王を祀る。旧社のあった八島町及びこれらの小社のある町は全て中世の八島郷に含まれていたようである<ref name="fudoki"/><ref>大和志料</ref><ref group="脚注">八島郷の範囲は、興隆寺・鉢伏・鹿野園・古市・安場・虚空蔵・高樋・山村・窪ノ庄・池田・今市・南永井・北永井・出屋敷・神殿・八島・横井・藤原・田中の19村。</ref>。
===出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社=== {{神社 |名称 = 崇道天皇神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sudo Tenno Shrine Deyashiki Honden Far,jpg.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = 奈良県奈良市出屋敷町28 |祭神 = 崇道天皇（早良親王） |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[10月13日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |位置 = {{coord|34|39|47.2|N|135|49|44.4|E}} }} {{osm box|w|417323039|出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社}} 760平方メートルの境内東方に石鳥居があり、西に進むと南面する本殿が見える<ref name="shishishaji511">奈良市史 社寺編 p.511</ref>。本殿のみで境内社はない<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。周辺は境内にすぐ隣接して住宅地となるが、境内にはまだ多くの樹木が残り、遠方からでも森を望むことができる<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。玉垣外に一対の石灯籠があり、「[[天明]]元年（[[1781年]]） 崇道天王 清水永井」と刻まれている<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。玉垣内の石灯籠、狛犬は[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）製<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。
===神殿町 崇道天皇神社=== {{神社 |名称 = 崇道天皇神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sudo Tenno Shrine Kodono Haiden,jpg.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = 奈良県奈良市神殿町522 |祭神 = 崇道天皇（早良親王） |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = 銅板葺 |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |位置 = {{coord|34|39|31.8|N|135|49|24.0|E}} }} {{osm box|w|417320208|神殿町 崇道天皇神社}} 本殿は銅板葺で南面し、各部の金具はりっぱな造りになっている<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。古くから雨の神として信仰され、[[江戸時代|江戸期]]末の[[絵馬]]が残されている。境内社として[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]が祀られている他、本殿築地の外、拝殿の南の東側には稲荷神社もあり、[[保食神]]を祀っている<ref name="shishishaji511"/>。稲荷の北には[[地蔵菩薩|地蔵]]堂が安置され、小さな石仏が壇上に並ぶ。堂の傍らには五輪塔の火輪、蓮座等が置かれているが、隣接する神殿町公民館はかつて安養寺と呼ばれる寺院であったため、その遺物と思われる<ref name="shishishaji512">奈良市史 社寺編 p.512</ref>。
===北永井町 崇道天皇神社=== {{神社 |名称 = 崇道天皇神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sudo Tenno Shrine KitaNagai Haiden.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = 奈良県奈良市北永井町425 |祭神 = 崇道天皇（早良親王） |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |位置 = {{coord|34|39|21.6|N|135|49|30.0|E}} }} {{osm box|w|417317860|北永井町 崇道天皇神社}} 本殿は南面し、左右に市杵島神社（[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]）と八阪神社（[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]）が鎮座している<ref name="shishishaji512"/>。他に拝殿、土蔵等があり、境内はかなり広く、拝殿南の境内地にはすべり台など遊具が設けてある<ref name="shishishaji512"/>。[[宮座]]は元、東・中・西など五座があったが、のちに本[[頭屋|当屋]]・平当屋・新当屋などを中心に祭事が行われ、それも次第に簡略化されている<ref name="shishishaji512"/>。
==脚注== {{Reflist|group="脚注"|2}}
==出典== {{Reflist|2}}
==参考文献== * {{Cite book|和書|editor =「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会 竹内理三|year = 1990|title =[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 29 奈良県|page=|publisher = 角川書店|isbn = 4040012909}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=[[池田源太]]、[[宮坂敏和]]、奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=[[名著出版]]|year=1989|page=|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 考古編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1968|page=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1985|page=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=大和古代地名辞典|editor=[[池田末則]]、日本地名学研究所|publisher=[[五月書房]]|year=1998|page=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=〈小さき社〉の列島史|editor=[[牛山佳幸]]|publisher=平凡社|year=2000|page=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良町風土記 続編|editor=[[山田熊夫]]|publisher=豊住書店|year=1979|page=68-69}}
==関連項目== * [[崇道天皇神社]] - 各地にある早良親王を祀る神社へのリンク。 * [[御霊神社]] * [[御霊信仰]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|location={{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.647306|N|135.841642|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24875285]] Wikidata name: Yashima no Misasagi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>
== References == {{Reflist|group="脚注"|2}}
== Source Material == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yashima no Misasagi}} * [http://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/index.php?m=d&id=28 崇道天皇八島陵 - 観光スポット｜奈良市観光協会公式ホームページ]

'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

<!--Yashima Mausoleum--> [[Category:History of Nara]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Yashima no Misasagi}} {{wikidata link|Q24875285}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|location={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.647306|N|135.841642|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24875285]] Wikidata name: Yashima no Misasagi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>

A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>

=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>

He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|location={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.647306|N|135.841642|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24875285]] Wikidata name: Yashima no Misasagi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>

He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|location={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.647306|N|135.841642|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24875285]] Wikidata name: Yashima no Misasagi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|location={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.647306|N|135.841642|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24875285]] Wikidata name: Yashima no Misasagi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者=[[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
