=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>

A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>

He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>

A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It was officially designated during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei]] 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|location={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.647306|N|135.841642|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24875285]] Wikidata name: Yashima no Misasagi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者={{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>
== References == {{Reflist|group="脚注"|2}}
== Source Material == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yashima no Misasagi}} * [http://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/index.php?m=d&id=28 崇道天皇八島陵 - 観光スポット｜奈良市観光協会公式ホームページ]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|location={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.647306|N|135.841642|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}}} {{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者={{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>
== References == {{Reflist|group="脚注"|2}}
== Source Material == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yashima no Misasagi}} * [http://narashikanko.or.jp/spot/index.php?m=d&id=28 崇道天皇八島陵 - 観光スポット｜奈良市観光協会公式ホームページ]

'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|1=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei|1=Kan'ei}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yashima no Misasagi]][[ja:八島陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八島陵]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|location={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.647306|N|135.841642|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}}} {{Nihon no kofun |名称=Yashima Tomb (八島陵) |image=[[File:Yashima misasagi.jpg|250px]] |所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} 八島町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 50.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 29.91 |ISO = JP-29 |type={{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |規模= |築造年代= |埋葬施設= |被葬者={{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} |burial goods= |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 八島陵 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 円墳 }} {{osm box|w|417313284}} [[File:Yashima misasagi from far.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the base of the approach]]
'''Yashima Tomb''' (八島陵), also known as '''Yashima no misasagi''' or '''Sudo Tenno Tomb''' (崇道天皇陵), is the mausoleum of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It was officially designated during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} era.<ref name="kadokawa1113">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1113</ref>
== Structure == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and the '''Shoryoshiki''' (諸陵式), the tomb site is recorded to measure "East-West five chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 545 meters</ref>, North-South four chō<ref group="脚注">Approximately 436 meters</ref>, with two smoke guards."<ref name="shishikoko193">奈良市史 考古編, p.193</ref> Later measurements in the 8th edition of {{ill|Nara City Archaeological Report|ja|奈良市報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Archaeological Report|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} show smaller dimensions of "East-West 1 chō, 1 bu, 2 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 109 meters</ref>, and North-South 38 ken, 1 bu, 7 rin<ref group="脚注">Approximately 69 meters</ref>."<ref name="shishikoko193"/> Its shape is slightly oval, enclosed by rectangular earthen walls, and surrounded by dense matsu and broad-leaved trees, giving it the appearance of a rectangular mound from afar.<ref name="shishikoko193"/> The '''Ryoho Yoran''' (陵墓要覧) classifies it as a round tumulus.<ref name="kadokawa1113"/>
=== Yashima Tomb's Front Stone Chamber === [[File:Yashima_misasagi_Yatsuishi.jpg|thumb|180px|Yatsuishi, or Eight Stones]] {{osm box|w|417313278|八つ石}} Near the roadway in front of the tomb, a late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} tumulus can be found, with its covering soil eroded to reveal large natural stones forming the ceiling of a horizontal stone chamber.<ref name="shishikoko195">奈良市史 考古編, p.195</ref> Measuring approximately 7 meters in length and 3 meters in width, the stones are known locally as "Yatsuishi" (Eight Stones). A local legend recounts that Prince Sawara, as he died in {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}, cast nine stones, asking to be buried where they landed.<ref name="kadokawa1112">角川日本地名大辞典 29 奈良県, p.1112</ref><ref name="fudoki">奈良町風土記 続編, pp.68-69</ref><ref name="yatsuishi">Yamato Province 添上郡八嶋陵 八嶋明神八ッ石明神原因記</ref> Eight of these were found in this area, inspiring the tomb's construction and possibly the origin of the area's name.<ref name="kadokawa1112"/><ref name="fudoki"/><ref name="yatsuishi"/> However, archaeological evidence indicates the tomb predates Prince Sawara, being from the late Kofun period.<ref name="shishikoko196">奈良市史 考古編, p.196</ref> Attempts to remove the stones allegedly resulted in mysterious deaths or illnesses among workers, leading to rumors of a curse.<ref>八島町資料集</ref><ref name="kadokawa1112"/>
== History == Prince Sawara was accused of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|1=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and stripped of his title in {{ill|Enryaku 4|ja|延暦4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (785). He died at age 35 while being exiled to {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}, where his body was initially interred.<ref>続日本紀</ref><ref name="shishikoko194">奈良市史 考古編, p.194</ref> Reports of his spirit causing calamities led to his posthumous elevation to Emperor Sudo in Enryaku 19 (800) and the designation of his tomb as a mausoleum.<ref name="ruiju">類聚国史</ref><ref name="kadokawa1113"/> Five years later, in Enryaku 24 (805), his remains were transferred to Yashima Tomb.<ref>日本後紀</ref><ref name="ruiju"/><ref name="kadokawa1113"/><ref name="chiisaki">小さき社の列島史, p.124</ref>
In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei|1=Kan'ei}} 4 (1627), records in the ''{{ill|Sokokushi|ja|宗国史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokokushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' mention the existence of '''Emperor Sudo Shrine''' and a temple near the tomb.<ref name="shishishaji545">奈良市史 社寺編, p.545</ref> These structures were later removed or relocated during the modernization of the tomb site in the Meiji period.<ref name="shishishaji545"/>
